PARANOIA 
Only gamemasters are cleared to read the LUttraviolet sections. Naturaly, it would be silly ta sell someone a game and tell him not to read it, but there are two things we'd like you to do: 
1. Don't read the adventure! Only. the gamemaster is supposed to do so. Reading it will ruin your enjoyment when you play it If you do read it, fess up to the GM and either, don’t play that adventure (there's always Another time), of ty to stop yourself from tak ing advantage of what you know when you play, Let the other players try to figure out ‘what's going on without the benefit of your superior knowledge. 
2. Lots of citizens of Alpha Complex know more than they should, Infact, knowing things, that it’s treason for you to know is probably necessary if you're going to survive. However, Citizens always do thelr utmost to hide their tweasonous knowledge. That should be your tule. Go ahead; read the Ultraviolet sections But any time you reveal your knowledge of their contents during play, you'll earn a weason, point. No doubt, even if you didn't read the Section, you'd develop a knowledge of the combal system over time — but talk about the details of the system in your first adventure, and you'll soon find yourslt staring dawn the bores of some very large weapons, 
New Clones 
You're Mod-R:NRTI, and Fred¥LKR blows you away just because you stole his syn- thochoc ration. The Computer dispatches your next clone, You're still annoyed at Fred, so Mod-R-NRT2 opens fire No! No! Stop it. Yes, Mod-R-NRTF2 has the same personality and abilities as Mod-R-NRT, but he'is a different person. You may still be annoyed at Fred's player, but your character, Mo-R-NRF2, cannot “stil” be angry, because hhe wasn’t around when Mod-R-NRTI was killed, Mod-R-NRF2 has no cause to open fre. If you really want revenge, get it in a more subtle fashion, Keep an eye on Fred and catch him doing something treasonous, or plant evidence on him, or something. 
Mod-R'NRF2 proved to have the mutant power of telekinesis, and was executed for his treason, Mod-R-NRT3. shows up. Since the players know he has the same power and is therefore a traitor, they open fire Waitt Stop! Cut! Take to, The players may know that Mod-R-NRT3 has telekinesis, but their characters do not, Mak- ing every clone identical is a game corven- lence; It saves a lot of time generating new characters, and is the basis for some cheap

You can start to play now, if you like, Ia pamemaster and other players aren't handy, you can play the solitaire adventure on pages D1 through 24, It will teach you a little more about Alpha Complex, and show you how the frame concepis work in practice ‘You can also read “Tips for Traitors” pages 25 through 28. SERVE THE COMPUTER! THE COMPUTER IS YOUR FRIEND 
Computer, and enough about the rules to play ‘Admittedly, you don't know very much, but that is the key to suspense and adventure in Paranoia. Life can be very exciting when you don't know who your enemies are. A suc cessful Troubleshooter develops a healthy sense of insecurity. However, you can't let paranoia paralyze you. You must survive. You must use wit, in- tuition and daring to rise to higher levels of power and prestige. There you may learn the secrets forbidden by your security clearance. 
SERVICE WILL BE REWARDED 
jokes. However, the fact that all clones have the same power is not known to the citizens ‘of Alpha Complex, or to The Computer. Just because you know that Mod-R-NRT3 is a traitor does not mean your character does. Act accordingly. (Or... if the players uncover one clone’s 
power, and the gamemaster thinks it is too destructive of the game to give the next clone the same power, he may very well decide, in this special case, to give the next clone a dif- ferent power, So don't assume that just because the rules say all clones have the same power that i's necessarily so, 
Paranoia often purposefully blurs the line between the player and his character. However, in many cases, its important to be aware of the differences, and to not let your feelings control your character's actions — or your character's feelings affect the way you treat the ‘other players. Realize that each clone stars off fresh, and don't carry over one clone’s an- tipathies to the next one, Realize that because ‘one players character has betrayed yours is no, reason to get upset at that player — betrayal is all part of the game.
